blocks:
Surah Al-Insan

And it is called Surah Ad-Dahr. It was said: It is entirely Meccan. And it was said: It is entirely Medinan. And it was said: It is Medinan except His saying: وَلا تُطِعْ ** (and do not obey) the Ayah. And it consists of thirty-one Ayahs* by consensus. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ*** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(1) هَلْ أَتى عَلَى الْإِنْسانِ ** (1) Has there been a time for man This is an interrogative (استفهام) for affirmation (تقرير) and approximation (تقريب), and therefore it was interpreted as (قد). حِينٌ مِنَ الدَّهْرِ ** a stretch of time a portion of time (طائفة من الزمان) لَمْ يَكُنْ شَيْئاً مَذْكُوراً. when he was not a thing worthy of mention?

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "He was decreed (مقدوراً) but not mentioned (مذكوراً)."

And in al-Majma', he said: "He was a decreed thing (شيئاً مقدوراً) and was not yet formed (مكوّناً)."

And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: "He was a thing (شيئاً) but was not mentioned (مذكوراً)."

And similar to this is found in al-Mahasin, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and in al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): "He was mentioned (مذكوراً) in [Divine] knowledge (العلم) but was not mentioned (مذكوراً) among creation (الخلق)."

(٢) إِنَّا خَلَقْنَا الْإِنْسانَ مِنْ نُطْفَةٍ أَمْشاجٍ ** (2) We created man from a drop of mixed fluid, mixtures (اخلاط). Al-Qummi narrated from Al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The fluid of the man and the woman, mixed together. ** نَبْتَلِيهِ ** that We may test him. We test him (نختبره). ** فَجَعَلْناهُ سَمِيعاً بَصِيراً ** So We made him hearing and seeing. So that he may be enabled to listen to the signs (الآيات) and observe the proofs (الدّلائل).

(٣) إِنَّا هَدَيْناهُ السَّبِيلَ ** (3) We have indeed shown him the way**

by establishing the proofs (الدلائل) and sending down the signs (الآيات).

Al-Qummi (said): meaning, We clarified for him the path of good and evil.

إِمَّا شاكِراً وَإِمَّا كَفُوراً. whether grateful or ungrateful.

In al-Kafi and al-Tawhid, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: We made him aware, whether taking (it) or leaving (it).

And al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): whether taking (it), then he is grateful (شاكر), or leaving (it), then he is ungrateful (كافر).

(٤) إِنَّا أَعْتَدْنا لِلْكافِرِينَ سَلاسِلَ ** (4) Indeed We have prepared chains for the faithless, by which they are led, وَأَغْلالاً ** and shackles, by which they are bound, وَسَعِيراً ** and a blaze, by which they are burned. And it was recited (سلاسلاً) for the sake of consistency (للمناسبة).

(٥) إِنَّ الْأَبْرارَ يَشْرَبُونَ مِنْ كَأْسٍ ** (5) Indeed the righteous will drink from a cup of wine, and it is originally the goblet (القدح) in which it is contained, كانَ مِزاجُها ** whose mixture is that which is mixed with it, **كافُوراً ** camphor, due to its coolness, its sweetness, and the pleasantness of its fragrance.

(٦) عَيْناً يَشْرَبُ بِها عِبادُ اللهِ ** (6) a spring where Allah’s servants will drink, Al-Qummi [said]: meaning, from it, يُفَجِّرُونَها تَفْجِيراً ** making it gush forth as they please. They make it flow wherever they wish, an easy flow.

In al-Majalis, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It is a spring in the house of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, which is made to gush forth to the houses of the Prophets and the believers.

(٧) يُوفُونَ بِالنَّذْرِ ** (7) They fulfill their vows A clarification of that for the sake of which they were provided (sustenance). And this is more eloquent in describing them as being abundant in performing obligations (الواجبات), because whoever fulfills what he obligated upon himself is more likely to fulfill what Allah obligated upon Him. وَيَخافُونَ يَوْماً كانَ شَرُّهُ مُسْتَطِيراً and fear a day whose ill will be widespread. Its hardships (شدائده) are widespread (فاشياً), spreading (منتشراً) to the utmost extent of spreading (غاية الانتشار). Al-Qummi (said): al-mustaṭīr (المستطير) means the great (العظيم). And in al-Majalis, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says (it is) frowning (كلوحاً) and grimacing (عابساً).

(8) ** وَيُطْعِمُونَ الطَّعامَ عَلى حُبِّهِ ** (8) and feed the needy, the orphan and the prisoner for the love of Him. The love of food (حبّ الطعام). In al-Majalis, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says: [It means] despite their desire (شهوتهم) for the food and their preference (إيثارهم) for it. ** مِسْكِيناً ** He said: [It refers to] one of the poor (مساكين) among the Muslims. ** وَيَتِيماً ** He said: [It refers to] one of the orphans (يتامى) among the Muslims. ** وَأَسِيراً ** He said: [It refers to] one of the captives (أسارى) among the polytheists (المشركين).

(٩)** إِنَّما نُطْعِمُكُمْ لِوَجْهِ اللهِ لا نُرِيدُ مِنْكُمْ جَزاءً وَلا شُكُوراً ** (9) 'We feed you only for the sake of Allah; we desire no reward from you, nor thanks.'

He said: They say this when they feed them that (food). He said: By Allah, they did not say this to them, but they concealed it within themselves, and Allah informed (others) of their concealment (بإضمارهم). They say: We do not desire from you a reward (جزاء) by which you recompense (تكافئوننا) us, nor thanks (شكوراً) by which you praise (تثنون) us. But rather, we fed you for the sake of Allah (لوجه الله) and seeking His reward (ثوابه).

(١٠) ** إِنَّا نَخافُ مِنْ رَبِّنا يَوْماً عَبُوساً ** (10) We fear from our Lord a day, frowning, in which faces will frown ** قَمْطَرِيراً ** (frowning, severe). intensely frowning (شديد العبوس).

In al-Majma', the specific (الخاص) and the general (العام) have narrated that the verses from this Surah, which are His saying: ** إِنَّ الْأَبْرارَ يَشْرَبُونَ ** (5) The virtuous will drink) up to His saying: ** كانَ سَعْيُكُمْ مَشْكُوراً ** (22) your endeavor is thanked), were revealed concerning Ali, Fatima, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn (peace be upon them), and a maidservant of theirs named Fidda (فضّة).

The story is long, but its summary is that al-Hasan and al-Husayn fell ill, and their grandfather and the notables of the Arabs visited them. They said: "O Abu al-Hasan, if only you would make a vow (نذراً) for your two sons." So he vowed to fast for three days if Allah, the Glorified, cured them. Fatima (peace be upon her) also vowed, and likewise Fidda. They recovered, but they possessed nothing. So Ali (peace be upon him) borrowed three sa's (أصوع) of barley from a Jew. It is narrated that He took it to spin wool for him. He brought it to Fatima, who ground one sa' (صاعاً) of it and baked it into bread. Ali (peace be upon him) prayed the Maghrib prayer and brought the food near them. Then a poor person (مسكين) came to them, supplicating for them and asking them (for food), so they gave it to him, and they tasted nothing but water. When the second day came, she took one sa', ground it, baked it, and presented it to Ali (peace be upon him). Suddenly, an orphan (يتيم) was at the door asking for food, so they gave it to him, and they tasted nothing but water. When the third day came, she took the remainder, ground it, baked it, and presented it to Ali (peace be upon him). Suddenly, a captive (أسير) was at the door asking for food, so they gave it to him, and they tasted nothing but water. When the fourth day came, and they had fulfilled their vows (نذورهم), Ali (peace be upon him), accompanied by al-Hasan and al-Husayn (peace be upon them), came to the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), and they showed signs of weakness. The Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) wept, and Gabriel (peace be upon him) descended with Surah Hal Ata (هل اتى).

In a narration, Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) hired himself out to water date palms for a measure of barley one night until morning. When morning came and He received the barley, He ground one-third of it. They made a food from it to eat, called al-Harirah (الحريرة). When its cooking was complete, a poor person (مسكين) came, and they brought the food out to him. Then they prepared the second third. When its cooking was complete, an orphan (يتيم) came and asked, so they fed him. Then they prepared the third third. When its cooking was complete, a captive (أسير) from the polytheists (المشركين) came and asked, so they fed him, and they spent that day fasting (طووا يومهم ذلك).

And al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Fatima had some barley, so they made it into a porridge (عصيدة). When they had finished cooking it and placed it before them, a poor person (مسكين) came. The poor person said: "May Allah have mercy on you, feed us from what Allah has provided for you." Ali (peace be upon him) stood up and gave him one-third of it. Soon after, an orphan (يتيم) came. The orphan said: "May Allah have mercy on you." Ali (peace be upon him) stood up and gave him the one-third. Then a captive (أسير) came. The captive said: "May Allah have mercy on you." Ali (peace be upon him) gave him the remaining third, and they did not taste it. So Allah, the Glorified, revealed the verses concerning them, and this applies to every believer who does such a deed for Allah, the Mighty and Majestic.

And in al-Majalis, narrated from al-Sadiq, from his father (peace be upon them both): what is close to what was mentioned.

In al-Majma', in the first narration, with an elaboration of the speech and additions recounting their actions and sayings (peace be upon them), it is mentioned: "And the two boys said: 'We too will fast for three days.' So Allah clothed them in well-being (عافية), and they awoke fasting." And at its end: Gabriel descended and said: "O Muhammad, take what Allah has bestowed upon you concerning your Household (أهل بيتك)." He said: "And what shall I take, O Gabriel?" He said: ** هَلْ أَتَى ** (1) Has there come upon man) up to His saying: ** وَكانَ سَعْيُكُمْ مَشْكُوراً** (22) your endeavor is thanked).

And in al-Manaqib, narrated from more than twenty of the great exegetes (المفسّرين).

And by the narration of the Household (أهل البيت), from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), what is close to what was mentioned in al-Majalis, except that it does not mention the fasting of the two boys. And at its end: The Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) saw them hungry, so Gabriel descended, and with him was a platter (صفحة) of gold, inlaid with pearls (الدرّ) and rubies (الياقوت), filled with tharid (الثّريد) and a bone (عراق) from which the scent of musk (المسك) and camphor (الكافور) emanated. They sat and ate until they were satisfied (شيعوا), and not a single morsel was diminished from it. Al-Husayn went out, and with him was a piece of the bone (عراق). A Jewish woman called out to him: "O Household of hunger, where did you get this? Feed it to me." Al-Husayn (peace be upon him) extended his hand to feed her, but Gabriel descended and took it from his hand and raised the platter to the heaven. He said: "Were it not for what al-Husayn (peace be upon him) intended—to feed that piece to the girl—I would have left that platter with my Household (أهل بيتي), and they would eat from it until the Day of Resurrection." And يُوفُونَ بِالنَّذْرِ (7) who fulfill their vows) was revealed. The charity (الصدقة) occurred on the night of the twenty-fifth of Dhu al-Hijjah (ذي الحجّة), and (هَلْ أَتى) was revealed on the twenty-fifth day of it.

(١١) فَوَقاهُمُ اللهُ شَرَّ ذلِكَ الْيَوْمِ وَلَقَّاهُمْ نَضْرَةً وَسُرُوراً. (11) So Allah saved them from the evil of that day, and granted them freshness and joy. In al-Majalis, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): نَضْرَةً ** (freshness) is in the faces, and وَسُرُوراً ** (joy) is in the hearts. (١٢) وَجَزاهُمْ بِما صَبَرُوا جَنَّةً وَحَرِيراً ** (12) And requited them for their patience with a garden and silk. He said: A garden (جنّة) they inhabit, and silk (حريراً) they spread out and wear. (١٣) مُتَّكِئِينَ فِيها عَلَى الْأَرائِكِ ** (13) Reclining therein on couches, He said: The أريكة (couch) is the bed (السرير) upon which is the canopy (الحجلة). لا يَرَوْنَ فِيها شَمْساً وَلا زَمْهَرِيراً ** where they will see neither sun nor bitter cold. It was said: It means that a moderate air passes over them, neither scorching hot nor harmful cold. (١٤) دانِيَةً عَلَيْهِمْ ظِلالُها ** (14) Its shades will be close above them, Close to them. وَذُلِّلَتْ قُطُوفُها تَذْلِيلاً ** and its clusters will be hanging low, within reach. Easy to reach. Al-Qummi said: Its fruits are made subservient to them; the standing and the sitting person can reach them. And in al-Kafi, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: وَذُلِّلَتْ قُطُوفُها تَذْلِيلاً ** (and its clusters will be hanging low, within reach) means due to their closeness to them, so the believer takes the exact type of fruit he desires while he is reclining.

(١٥) وَيُطافُ عَلَيْهِمْ بِآنِيَةٍ مِنْ فِضَّةٍ وَأَكْوابٍ ** (15) They will be served around with vessels of silver and goblets. Al-Qummi said: The goblets (الأكواب) are large jugs (الأكواز العظام) which have no handles (آذان) nor loops (عرى). كانَتْ قَوارِيرَا. (which are) crystal. (١٦) قَوارِيرَا مِنْ فِضَّةٍ ** (16) crystal made of silver, meaning they combine the clarity and transparency of glass with the whiteness and softness of silver. In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) and Al-Qummi, he said: The sight penetrates the silver of Paradise just as it penetrates glass. And قَوَارِيرًا was read with tanwin (nunation) in both instances, or specifically in the first one. قَدَّرُوها تَقْدِيراً ** (and) they have measured them exactly. It was said that is, they measured them in their souls, so their sizes and shapes came out exactly as they wished; or they measured them by their righteous deeds, so they came according to them; or those who serve them measured their drink according to the measure of their desire. And Al-Qummi says: They were made for them according to the measure of their needs (تقلّبهم); there is neither restriction (تحجّر) nor excess (فضل) in them.

(١٧) وَيُسْقَوْنَ فِيها كَأْساً كانَ مِزاجُها زَنْجَبِيلاً (17) And they will be served therein a cup whose admixture is ginger, [It is] what resembles ginger (الزّنجبيل) in taste. It was said that the Arabs found pleasure in the drink mixed with it.

(١٨) عَيْناً فِيها (١) تُسَمَّى سَلْسَبِيلاً (18) a spring in it called Salsabīl. It was said [it is called سَلْسَبِيلاً] due to the smoothness (سلاسة) of its descent in the throat and the ease of its passage (مساغها), provided that the letter باء (ب) is considered extra (زائدة), and the intention behind it is to negate the pungency (لذع) of the ginger (الزنجبيل) from it.

In al-Khisal, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: "Allah granted me five things, and He granted Ali five things. He granted me al-Kawthar, and He granted him al-Salsabil."

(1) Meaning, the wine (الخمر) is mixed with ginger (الزنجبيل), and the ginger (الزنجبيل) is from a spring (عين) called Salsabīl (سلسبيلاً).

(١٩) ** وَيَطُوفُ عَلَيْهِمْ وِلْدانٌ مُخَلَّدُونَ ** (19) And immortal youths will serve them It was said: everlasting (دائمون). And al-Qummi said: wearing bracelets (مسورون). ** إِذا رَأَيْتَهُمْ حَسِبْتَهُمْ لُؤْلُؤاً مَنْثُوراً ** when you see them you will suppose them to be scattered pearls. due to the purity of their complexions, and their spreading out in their assemblies, and the reflection of the radiance of some of them upon others.

(٢٠) وَإِذا رَأَيْتَ ثَمَّ رَأَيْتَ نَعِيماً وَمُلْكاً كَبِيراً. (20) And when you look there, you will see bliss and a great kingdom.

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), in a narration describing the state of the believer when he enters the Gardens (الجنان) and the chambers (الغرف), he said regarding this Ayah: "He means by that the friend of Allah (وليّ الله) and what he possesses of honor (الكرامة), bliss (النعيم), and the great dominion (الملك العظيم). And indeed, the angels, who are messengers of Allah, seek permission to enter upon him, and they do not enter upon him except with his permission. That is the great dominion (الملك العظيم). The complete narration has already passed in [the commentary on] al-Ra'd, Fatir, and al-Zumar."

And in al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked, "What is this great kingdom (الملك الكبير) which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, magnified until He named it great?" He said: "When Allah admits the people of Paradise into Paradise, He sends a messenger to a friend (وليّ) among His friends (أوليائه). The messenger finds the gatekeepers (الحجبة) at his door, and they say to him: 'Stop until we seek permission for you.' The messenger of his Lord does not reach him except with his permission. That is His saying: وَإِذا رَأَيْتَ ثَمَّ رَأَيْتَ نَعِيماً وَمُلْكاً كَبِيراً. (And when you look there, you will see bliss and a great kingdom.)"

And in al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "Meaning, it does not cease and does not perish."

(٢١) ** عالِيَهُمْ ثِيابُ سُندُسٍ خُضْرٌ وَإِسْتَبْرَقٌ ** (21) Upon them will be garments of green silk and brocade. Upon them are garments of green silk, both that which is fine (ما رقّ منها) and that which is thick (وما غلظ). In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام and al-Qummi, he said: The garments are upon them, so they wear them. And it was read (وقرئ) عَالِيَهُمْ with raf' (بالرفع), and خُضْرٍ with jarr (بالجرّ), and إِسْتَبْرَقٌ with raf' (بالرّفع), and vice versa (وبالعكس), and with raf' in both (وبالرفع فيهما). وَحُلُّوا أَساوِرَ مِنْ فِضَّةٍ وَسَقاهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ شَراباً طَهُوراً. And they will be adorned with bracelets of silver, and their Lord will give them to drink a purifying drink. In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام in the previous hadith: "At the gate of Paradise is a tree, such that a thousand men among the people can seek shade beneath one of its leaves. To the right of the tree is a purifying, sanctifying spring (عين مطهّرة مزكّية)." He said: "So they are given a drink from it, and Allah purifies their hearts thereby from envy (الحسد), and the hair falls from their skins (أبشارهم). That is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: وَسَقاهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ شَراباً طَهُوراً ** (and their Lord will give them to drink a purifying drink) from that purifying spring (العين المطهّرة)." And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, he said: "He purifies them from everything other than Allah."

(٢٢) إِنَّ هذا كانَ لَكُمْ جَزاءً ** (22) This is indeed your reward, [This is] concerning the implied saying (القول), ** وَكانَ سَعْيُكُمْ مَشْكُوراً ** and your endeavor has been thanked. not wasted. (٢٣) إِنَّا نَحْنُ نَزَّلْنا عَلَيْكَ الْقُرْآنَ تَنْزِيلاً ** (23) Indeed We have sent down the Quran to you in a gradual sending down. separately (مفرّقاً), bit by bit (منجّماً). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), He said: [It refers to] the guardianship (بولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him). (٢٤) فَاصْبِرْ لِحُكْمِ رَبِّكَ ** (24) So be patient for the judgement of your Lord, regarding the delay of your victory over the enemies, ** وَلا تُطِعْ مِنْهُمْ آثِماً أَوْ كَفُوراً. and do not obey any sinner or ingrate among them. (٢٥) وَاذْكُرِ اسْمَ رَبِّكَ بُكْرَةً وَأَصِيلاً (25) And remember the Name of your Lord morning and evening, Al-Qummi said: [It means] in the morning (بالغداة) and at midday (ونصف النهار). (٢٦) وَمِنَ اللَّيْلِ فَاسْجُدْ لَهُ وَسَبِّحْهُ لَيْلاً طَوِيلاً ** (26) and prostrate to Him during the night, and glorify Him through a long night. He said: [This refers to] the night prayer (صلاة اللّيل). And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked, "And what is that glorification (التسبيح)?" He said: "The night prayer (صلاة اللّيل)." And it was said: ** بُكْرَةً ** [refers to] the Fajr prayer (صلاة الفجر), ** وَأَصِيلاً ** [refers to] the two noon prayers (الظهران) [Dhuhr and Asr], وَمِنَ اللَّيْلِ فَاسْجُدْ لَهُ [refers to] the two evening prayers (العشاءان) [Maghrib and Isha], ** وَسَبِّحْهُ لَيْلاً طَوِيلاً ** meaning: and perform the tahajjud prayer (تهجّد) for Him during a long portion (طائفة طويلة) of the night.

(٢٧) إِنَّ هؤُلاءِ يُحِبُّونَ الْعاجِلَةَ وَيَذَرُونَ وَراءَهُمْ ** (27) Indeed, these love the fleeting [life] and disregard a heavy day ahead of them. ahead of them or behind their backs a heavy day. (٢٨) نَحْنُ خَلَقْناهُمْ وَشَدَدْنا أَسْرَهُمْ ** (28) We created them and consolidated their frame, and We strengthened the binding of their joints with the sinews (بالاعصاب). al-Qummi (said): meaning their creation. وَإِذا شِئْنا بَدَّلْنا أَمْثالَهُمْ تَبْدِيلاً We destroyed them and replaced others like them in creation and the strength of the frame (الأسر), meaning the latter existence (النشأة الآخرة). And what is intended is replacing them with others from those who obey in this world (الدنيا).

(٢٩) إِنَّ هذِهِ تَذْكِرَةٌ فَمَنْ شاءَ اتَّخَذَ إِلى رَبِّهِ سَبِيلاً ** (29) This is indeed a reminder. So let anyone who wishes take the path toward his Lord. He draws near to Him through obedience. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), He said: The guardianship (الولاية). (٣٠) وَما تَشاؤُنَ إِلَّا أَنْ يَشاءَ اللهُ. (30) But you will not wish unless Allah wishes. In al-Khara'ij, narrated from al-Qa'im (peace be upon him): He was asked about the Mufawwidah (المفوّضة). He said: "They have lied. Rather, our hearts are vessels for the Will (مشيئة) of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ). So when He wills, we will." Then He recited this verse. And it was recited يَشَاؤُونَ (they wish) with the yā' (بالياء). إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ عَلِيماً حَكِيماً ** Indeed Allah is all-knowing, all-wise. He does not will except that which His knowledge (علمه) and His wisdom (حكمته) necessitate.

(٣١) يُدْخِلُ مَنْ يَشاءُ فِي رَحْمَتِهِ ** (He admits whomever He wishes into His mercy) through guidance (الهداية) and granting success (التوفيق) in obedience. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), he said: [It refers to] our Wilayah (ولاية). وَالظَّالِمِينَ أَعَدَّ لَهُمْ عَذاباً أَلِيماً. (and for the wrongdoers He has prepared a painful punishment.) In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Hal Ata 'ala al-Insan (Surah 76) every Thursday morning, Allah will marry him to eight hundred virgins (عذراء) and four thousand previously married women (ثيّب) from the Hur al-'In (الحور العين), and he will be with Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. And in al-Amali, narrated from al-Hadi (peace be upon him): Whoever loves that Allah protects him from the evil of Monday, let him recite Hal Ata 'ala al-Insan in the first rak'ah (ركعة) of the morning prayer, then let him recite فَوَقاهُمُ اللهُ شَرَّ ذلِكَ الْيَوْمِ ** (So Allah protected them from the evil of that day) the Ayah.
